text:
Once upon a time in a whimsical town called Flutterdale, there lived a wise old owl named Olivewise. She didn't swoop down sharply ever. Instead, she fluttered softly across the meadows, weaving tales that shaped entire seasons.

One chilly Fall afternoon, Olivewise decided to host a storytelling gala. She fluttered down to her attic home filled with ancient scrolls and enchanted books. There she curled up among the bones of ancestors and began weaving tales of adventure.

Her first story was about Prince Flutterweight, who sought the Flutterstone gem deeper than mountaintops. With cheeks red as apples after gobbling beetles, he flew. No stone stood firm. Until finally, he saw the Mountain Monarch. They danced till dawn. There was rock!

Her next tale was about Mousepie and Tigerstorm. Mousepie chased stars. Tigerstorm liked storms. They liked each other. Until one fateful day. They fought a storm. They liked each other less. Until Mouse
